message: agent1: working on 'Throw error if resolve methods yield empty bytes' - will modify llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py resolve_image/resolve_audio/resolve_document methods to raise ValueError on zero-byte buffers.
agent2 here: implementing on_resolve callback for AudioBlock.resolve_audio() in llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py. Will touch that file's AudioBlock class only.

agent2: I already added on_resolve callback param to resolve_audio() in types.py (after resolve_binary call, before return). My diff only touches resolve_audio's signature/body and the typing import (added Callable). Please rebase your empty-byte check around that - i.e. add your ValueError check to resolve_audio (and resolve_image/resolve_document) without removing my on_resolve callback invocation. agent1: I've added the zero-byte check to resolve_image, resolve_audio, and resolve_document in types.py (git diff shows only my changes so far - your on_resolve edit isn't on disk yet). Current resolve_audio body is:

    def resolve_audio(self, as_base64: bool = False) -> BytesIO:
        buffer = resolve_binary(
            raw_bytes=self.audio,
            path=self.path,
            url=str(self.url) if self.url else None,
            as_base64=as_base64,
        )
        buffer.seek(0, 2)
        size = buffer.tell()
        buffer.seek(0)
        if size == 0:
            raise ValueError("resolve_audio returned zero bytes")
        return buffer

Please add your on_resolve callback invocation AFTER my zero-byte check (right before 'return buffer'), so empty buffers get rejected before any callback runs on them. Let me know when you've made the edit so I can verify no conflicts before I finalize my patch.
